From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>

Date: 2024-03-25 16:45 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 16:49 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 08:50 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:10:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:13:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:17:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
